clinical_background: Borderline hyperlipidemia noted on an older lipid panel
Recent sleep disruption from work stress
Drop in daily activity over the last week
Resting heart rate drifted upward over 48 hours

details.narrative: Patient uploads an older blood test from January during onboarding because she wants the app to understand her baseline.
Patient connects her wearable and syncs the last 24 hours.
A second sync shows the pattern is persisting rather than being a one-off spike.
Cron notices the old cholesterol flag and the recent elevated resting heart rate pattern. The combined picture results in a medium-priority cardiovascular alert.
The summary job prepares a plain-language patient summary and a doctor-facing clinical summary.
Doctor logs in, sees Nisha in the patient list, opens her summary, and reviews the new alert.

details.question: Why did I get an alert?
details.expected_answer_shape: Grounded explanation referencing abnormal cholesterol history plus recent high resting heart rate and low activity trend.

doctor_patient_mapping.notes: Doctor must be mapped to patient before doctor dashboard and doctor summary access make sense.

alert.title: Cardiovascular risk trend needs review
reasoning: Historical lipid report shows LDL and total cholesterol above recommended range
Recent wearable sync shows elevated resting heart rate over consecutive days
Activity level and sleep duration have both dropped in the same period

doctor_summary_points: Past lipid abnormality remains clinically relevant: LDL 168 mg/dL and total cholesterol 244 mg/dL from uploaded historical panel.
Last 48-hour wearable pattern shows resting heart rate drifting from 86 bpm to 91 bpm with low activity and short sleep.
Current alert severity is medium because the signal suggests worsening cardiovascular strain but no acute critical threshold is documented.
patient_summary_points: Your earlier cholesterol report showed higher-than-ideal LDL and total cholesterol.
Your wearable data over the past two days also shows a faster resting pulse, less sleep, and lower activity.
That combination likely triggered the new medium alert so your care team can review the trend.
expected_outputs.voice_prompt: Why did I get an alert?

staging_seed_sequence.action: Create synthetic patient and doctor accounts
Run alerts evaluation cron or admin endpoint
Verify patient voice query answer is grounded
